name: Traditional Kashmiri Chilli Powder
description: Sun-dried Kashmiri chillies ground in slow-rotating stone chakkis. This keeps the grinding temperature low, locking in the natural red pigment and the volatile capsaicin oils. Offers a mild heat with a vibrant, deep red color and a sweet, smoky aroma.
benefits: Rich in Vitamin C and antioxidants
Locks natural oils without artificial colors or preservatives
usage: Perfect for curries, tandoori marinades, stews, and soups where a rich red color is desired with a gentle warmth.
recipes: Add 1 tsp to tandoori marinades for natural coloring.
Stir into slow-cooked gravies at the end to lock in the aroma.

name: High-Curcumin Hand-Pounded Turmeric Powder
description: Premium turmeric rhizomes sun-dehydrated and hand-pounded in traditional wooden and stone mortar-pestles (Okhli). Hand-pounding prevents thermal degradation, preserving the active compound Curcumin (exceeding 5.2%) and natural turmeric oils.
benefits: Super-high Curcumin content (>5.2%) for powerful anti-inflammatory support
Natural immunity booster with strong antimicrobial properties
Pure yellow golden texture without lead chromate or starch fillers
usage: Use in daily cooking, traditional milk elixirs (Golden Milk), skincare masks, or natural wellness shots.
recipes: Golden Milk: Whisk 1/2 tsp turmeric in hot milk with black pepper and honey.
Daily Tadka: Add 1/2 tsp to heated ghee/oil to activate curcumin with fat.

name: Stone-Ground Coriander Powder
description: Freshly selected coriander seeds (Dhania) dry-roasted in sun-baked clay pans, then stone-ground in traditional rotating hand-mills. Retains high concentrations of Linalool, the volatile compound responsible for the citrusy, herbal fragrance.
benefits: Excellent source of dietary fiber and minerals
Helps regulate blood sugar and digestion
Fresh, cooling digestive spice with zero metal dust contamination
usage: A cooling base spice for gravies, vegetable stir-fries, lentil dal, and traditional spice rubs.
recipes: Stir 1 tbsp into dals and curries along with cumin for a balanced earthiness.
Blend with cumin and black pepper for a homemade garam masala.
